1→--- 2→title: Workflow Run List 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying workflow run list display" 5→ - "working with erk workflow run list" 6→ - "understanding run-name format parsing" 7→ - "modifying workflow run display" 8→--- 9→ 10→# Workflow Run List 11→ 12→The `erk workflow run list` command displays GitHub Actions workflow runs in a PR-centric table view. 13→ 14→## Command Hierarchy 15→ 16→Moved from top-level `erk run` to `erk workflow run list` and `erk workflow run logs` in PR #8549. 17→ 18→## Source Files 19→ 20→- `src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py` -- Main list command (192 lines) 21→- `src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py` -- Shared utilities (42 lines) 22→- `src/erk/cli/commands/doctor_workflow.py:253` -- Registration point 23→ 24→## PR-Centric Display 25→ 26→Runs are displayed with PR numbers extracted from run-name format. The table columns are: run-id, status, submitted, workflow, pr, title, chks. 27→ 28→## Run-Name Format Parsing 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py:6-23 -->
... [50 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: The workflow-run-list.md file doesn't need changes - `pr_number` is correct in that context (it's referring to the workflow input variable name `pr_number`). Now let me verify the changes I made and create a summary:
session.tool_result:
140→4. Cleans up `.erk/impl-context/` staging directory (git rm + commit + push) 141→ 142→**Flags:** 143→ 144→- `--issue` - Plan number to fetch plan from 145→- `--file` - Local markdown file path 146→- `--no-impl` - Create branch only, skip `.erk/impl-context/` folder creation 147→ 148→**Output:** JSON with `success`, `source`, `pr_number`, `has_plan_tracking`, `valid`, `related_docs` 149→ 150→#### setup-impl-from-pr 151→ 152→Creates implementation environment from a plan: 153→ 154→1. Fetches plan from GitHub 155→2. Creates/checks out implementation branch (e.g., `P123-feature-01-15-1430`) 156→3. Creates `.erk/impl-context/` folder with plan content 157→4. Saves plan reference for PR linking 158→ 159→**Flags:** 160→ 161→- `--no-impl` - Create branch only, skip `.erk/impl-context/` folder creation 162→ 163→**Branch behavior:** 164→ 165→- If branch exists: Checks out existing branch 166→- If on trunk: Creates branch from trunk 167→- If on feature branch: Stacks new branch on current branch 168→ 169→**Important:** After `create_branch()`, explicit `checkout_branch()` is called because GraphiteBranchManager restores the original branch after tracking.
... [55 lines omitted] ...
202→Batch commands read JSON arrays from stdin and process items individually. They continue on individual failures and always exit with code 0. 207→Both use frozen dataclass results with discriminated union error handling.

1→--- 2→title: erk pr prepare Command 3→read_when: 4→ - "setting up impl-context for an existing PR" 5→ - "preparing a worktree for plan implementation" 6→ - "working with erk pr prepare" 7→--- 8→ 9→# erk pr prepare Command 10→ 11→Sets up `.erk/impl-context/` for the current worktree's PR, making it ready for plan implementation without full checkout. 12→ 13→## Usage 14→ 15→```bash 16→erk pr prepare # Auto-detect PR from current branch 17→erk pr prepare <number> # Prepare for specific plan number 18→``` 19→ 20→## Implementation 21→ 22→**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/prepare_cmd.py` 23→ 24→### Auto-Detection 25→ 26→When `number` is omitted, uses `ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch()` to find the PR associated with the current branch. 27→ 28→### Idempotency 29→ 30→Before creating impl-context, reads any existing impl directory and compares the plan ID. If the plan ID matches, skips setup — safe to re-run.
... [10 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Perfect! The changes have been applied successfully. Now let me verify all the TUI docs don't need changes by double-checking one more time:
session.tool_result: plan_id: GitHub issue number (e.g., 123). Never None. plan_url: Full URL to the GitHub issue. None when unavailable. plan_id: int plan_url: str | None
session.assistant:
Excellent! The TUI source code confirms that `plan_id` and `plan_url` are still the field names (they were NOT renamed). So all the TUI docs are correct as-is. Let me create a final summary of my verification and changes:
## Summary
I've completed a thorough review and update of the documentation files as requested. Here's what I found and changed:
### TUI Documentation Files (No Changes Needed)
All TUI documentation files are **correct as-is** because the TUI source code still uses `plan_id` and `plan_url` in `PlanRowData`:
1. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/data-contract.md` — Correctly documents `plan_id` and `plan_url` 2. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/plan-row-data.md` — Correctly documents `plan_id` 3. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/architecture.md` — Correctly references `plan_id` 4. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/action-inventory.md` — No field references 5. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/view-switching.md` — No field references 6. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/frozen-dataclass-field-management.md` — Correctly references `plan_id` in examples 7. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/runs-tab-architecture.md` — No problematic references 8. ✅ `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/clipboard-text-generation.md` — No problematic references
### CLI Documentation Files (Changes Made)
The JSON output from exec scripts changed `"plan_number"` → `"pr_number"` for PR-related fields. Updated:
1. **Updated** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/erk-exec-commands.md` - Line 148: `"plan_number"` → `"pr_number"` in `impl-init` output description - Line 204: `{"plan_number": int, ...}` → `{"pr_number": int, ...}` for `add-plan-labels` - Line 205: `{"plan_number": int, ...}` → `{"pr_number": int, ...}` for `close-prs`
2. **Updated** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/commands/pr-prepare.md` - Line 26: `plan_number` → `number` (parameter name in docs now matches command signature)
### Files Verified (No Changes Needed)
- `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/output-styling.md` — Uses `plan_id` which is correct, also notes `plan_context.plan_id` is still used - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/pr-submit-pipeline.md` — Uses `plan_id` which is correct - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md` — Uses `plan_id` which is correct - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/learn-plan-land-flow.md` — Uses `plan_id` which is correct - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/workflow-run-list.md` — Uses `pr_number` which is correct (workflow input variable) - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/exec-command-patterns.md` — Uses `plan_number` in markdown example (documentation context, not JSON) - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/ambiguity-resolution.md` — Uses `plan_number` in variable names (not JSON output)
All changes have been applied to ensure documentation accuracy matches the actual renamed JSON fields in the source code.
